data_sources: National Weather Service Severe Weather Data Inventory
data_quality: High - Government sources with established QA/QC
notes: Climate data (temperature, precipitation, extreme events) available via NOAA APIs. Flood data via FEMA GIS services. Wildfire data from NIFC. Sea level rise data from NOAA. Most data available in real-time or near real-time. IPCC scenario data requires manual integration.

data_quality: High - Census and EPA data are authoritative
notes: Demographic data (race, income, age, employment) readily available via Census API at block group level. EJScreen provides pre-calculated environmental justice metrics. Transit data via GTFS. Some local data (crime statistics) may require manual collection.

data_quality: High - EPA eGRID is the standard for grid emission factors
notes: Operational carbon data from energy models. Grid emission factors from EPA eGRID (updated annually). Embodied carbon from EC3 database with API access. Refrigerant GWP values from EPA. Most data can be automated through API integrations.

data_quality: Medium - Depends on project documentation quality
notes: Primarily relies on internal project data. No external APIs required. Template generation can be automated but content is project-specific. LEED prerequisite requirements are static and can be hardcoded.

data_quality: Medium - Highly dependent on project team input
notes: Mostly process-based credit requiring human coordination. Some data (energy targets, benchmark data) available from NREL. Goal tracking can be automated but charrette facilitation requires human involvement.

Green Lease Leaders criteria are published and static. LEED prerequisite requirements are known. Lease clause generation can be templated. Compliance checking can be automated against known requirements.

data_quality: High - Government GIS data with established accuracy
notes: All major data layers available via GIS web services. FEMA flood maps, NWI wetlands, USFWS critical habitat, and USDA farmland data all accessible programmatically. Slope analysis from USGS elevation data. Automated site screening is highly feasible.

Brownfield status available from EPA. Historic district data from National Park Service. Employment and housing data from Census/HUD. Local workforce training data may require manual verification. Apprenticeship tracking requires project-specific data.

Density calculations from assessor data. Transit accessibility from GTFS feeds (2000+ feeds globally). Walk Score has API. All data can be fetched programmatically. Some local assessor APIs may have rate limits.

data_quality: Medium-High - Mix of static and dynamic sources
notes: ITE parking data is static (published manual). Regional VMT data available from MPOs. EPA emission factors from eGRID. Some transportation data requires local coordination. Parking reduction calculations can be automated.

EVSE specifications from ENERGY STAR. Electrical load calculations can be automated. ADA compliance is rule-based. Most calculations are straightforward with project data. No external real-time data required.

data_quality: Medium - Mix of authoritative and project data
notes: Native plant and invasive species data available from USDA. EPA CGP requirements are static. Site-specific vegetation surveys require field work. Erosion control plans can be templated but require site-specific adaptation.

data_sources: Native plant databases (Lady Bird Johnson Wildflower Center)
data_quality: Medium - Some scientific data, some manufacturer data
notes: Native plant recommendations available from databases. Bird collision threat factors published in research. Glass specifications from manufacturers. Area calculations can be automated from site plans. Restoration specifications require design input.

Primarily design-based calculations from project drawings. ADA standards are static. Area calculations and seating requirements can be automated. Community/cultural elements require qualitative assessment.

data_quality: High - NOAA and USDA data are authoritative
notes: Rainfall data from NOAA Atlas 14 available via API. Soil infiltration data from USDA NRCS. Stormwater calculations (TR-55) can be fully automated. LID/GI sizing tools available. Some local requirements may vary.

data_quality: Medium - Depends on IPp1 data quality
notes: Relies heavily on IPp1 hazard assessment. Resilience strategies can be matched to hazards from a pre-defined database. SITES rating system provides additional guidance. Design implementation requires professional judgment.

data_quality: High - CRRC ratings are third-party verified
notes: Tree Equity Score available for US locations. CRRC database provides SRI values for roofing materials. Material specifications can be verified against databases. Weighted calculations can be fully automated.

BUG ratings are standardized. Manufacturer data typically available via product databases. Compliance checking is rule-based and can be fully automated. Lighting zone determination may require local jurisdiction lookup.
Meter specifications from manufacturers. Arc platform has API for data submission. Plumbing system data from design. Real-time metering data available via utility APIs or building management systems.

ENERGY STAR and WaterSense databases available. Fixture flow rates from manufacturer data. Irrigation calculations can use EPA TIR methodology. Compliance checking can be fully automated against product specifications.

Submeter placement can be automated from plumbing design. Leak detection sensor specifications from manufacturers. Threshold calculations (80%, 100%, etc.) are rule-based. Action plan generation can be templated.

Whole-building water modeling can be automated. Alternative water source availability from local data. Rainfall data from NOAA. Water reuse regulations vary by jurisdiction. Optimization pathways can be calculated.

credit_name: Operational Carbon Projection and Decarbonization Plan
notes: Grid emission factors from EPA eGRID (updated annually). Energy model integration possible via standardized formats. Decarbonization pathways can be modeled using NREL data. 25-year projections can be automated.

ASHRAE 90.1 requirements are static and can be hardcoded. Energy model comparison to baseline can be automated. Prescriptive compliance checking is rule-based. Performance path requires energy modeling software integration.

ASHRAE standards are static. Templates for OPR, BOD can be generated. Milestone tracking can be automated. However, commissioning is fundamentally a process requiring human execution. Documentation management can be automated.

Metering requirements are standardized. Arc platform has API for automated reporting. Utility data increasingly available via Green Button standard. BMS integration can provide real-time energy data. 5-year reporting can be automated.

Refrigerant regulations from EPA SNAP. GWP values from IPCC/EPA. Equipment data from AHRI directory. Compliance checking is rule-based (no CFCs, phase-out schedules). Can be fully automated with equipment schedules.

Equipment COP data from AHRI directory. Weighted average calculations can be automated. Climate zone determination from location. Electrification pathway analysis can be modeled. Equipment specification requires design input.

Peak load data from energy models. Envelope analysis can be automated. ERV sizing from manufacturer data. Thermal bridge detection requires detailed analysis. Compliance verification can be automated with model integration.

Percent improvement calculated from energy model comparison to baseline. ASHRAE 90.1 Appendix G defines baseline requirements. Prescriptive compliance is rule-based. Points calculation can be automated. Optimization recommendations require analysis.

Solar production estimates from PVWatts API. Solar resource data from NSRDB. REC verification via Green-e registry. On-site renewable percentage calculations can be automated. Solar-ready requirements are design-based.

data_quality: Medium - Mix of standards and building data
notes: Enhanced Cx requirements are standardized. MBCx analytics can use real-time BMS data. Fault detection algorithms can be automated. Performance tracking requires building sensor integration. Enclosure commissioning is process-based.

DR program availability from utility APIs. Storage sizing from manufacturer data. Load profiles from energy models or BMS. Smart grid integration can be verified against standards. OpenADR enables automated DR.

GWP values from IPCC/EPA. Equipment data from AHRI. Total GWP impact calculations can be fully automated. Low-GWP alternatives can be recommended from databases. Leak detection requirements are rule-based.

data_quality: Medium - Mix of benchmarks and local data
notes: Waste generation rates from EPA benchmarks. Storage area sizing can be calculated. Service availability requires local verification. Zero waste plan templates can be generated. Design details are project-specific.

EC3 database has API access with 20,000+ EPDs. Material baselines from CLF. EPDs from multiple registries. BIM integration enables automated quantity take-off. Hot spot identification can be automated. Reduction strategies can be recommended.

Existing building data requires field survey. Salvage material values from market data. Area calculations can be automated. Reuse percentage tracking can be automated. Material sourcing requires project coordination.

WBLCA tools (Tally, One Click LCA) have extensive databases. EC3 API provides EPD data. EPD analysis can be automated. Construction emission tracking from project data. Percent reduction calculations are straightforward.

GREENGUARD and FloorScore databases available online. CDPH standards are static. Product compliance can be verified against certification databases. Percentage calculations by category can be automated. HPDs available via repository API.

EPD registries have searchable databases. HPD repository has API. FSC certificate database is online. Certification validation can be automated. Product counting by category can be automated. Multiple pathways can be calculated.

data_quality: Medium - Depends on hauler reporting accuracy
notes: Waste tracking requires hauler coordination. Diversion rates from facility certifications. Material stream reporting can be automated. Compliance documentation can be generated. Real-time tracking possible with digitized systems.

data_quality: Medium - Mix of real-time and project data
notes: Heat index monitoring available via OSHA app and weather APIs. Construction IAQ plan templates can be generated. Schedule integration possible. Material delivery tracking requires coordination. Weather data is real-time.

ASHRAE 62.1 requirements are static. Outdoor air quality from EPA AirNow API. Ventilation rate calculations can be automated. Filtration verification (MERV 13+) is rule-based. Entryway design requirements are prescriptive.

Signage location planning can be automated from drawings. 25-foot buffer zones can be calculated. Policy document templates can be generated. Compliance verification is rule-based. No external data required.

Ventilation increase calculations (15%+) are straightforward. Enhanced IAQ limits are published. Compliance verification can be automated. Points achievable can be calculated. Requires HVAC design data.

Daylight analysis requires simulation (IES LM-83). View analysis can be automated from BIM. Acoustic mapping requires modeling. Thermal comfort analysis via ASHRAE 55. Multiple options with different data requirements.

data_quality: Medium - Mix of standards and best practices
notes: ADA standards are static. Enhanced accessibility checklist can be generated. Gap analysis can be automated against standards. Inclusive design features require qualitative assessment. Wayfinding requires design review.

Relies on IPp1 hazard assessment. Resilient design strategies can be matched to hazards. Operable window coverage can be calculated. Thermal safety modeling requires energy analysis. Management mode requirements are design-based.

Testing protocols can be generated from LEED requirements. Sensor specifications from manufacturers. Measurement locations can be planned. Thresholds are published. Continuous monitoring provides real-time data.

Regional Priority credits available from USGBC. Project Priority Library is published. Pilot credits cataloged. Exemplary performance calculations can be automated. Innovation strategies require project-specific development.
GBCI maintains credential directory. LEED AP status can be verified online. Specialty appropriateness can be checked. Credential status reporting can be automated. Simple API integration possible.
